title: Arnol'd's Chord Conjecture for Conormal Legendrian Lifts
speaker: Filip Broćić
abstract: The chord conjecture, due initially to Arnol'd in the case of the standard contact three-sphere, asserts the existence of a Reeb chord with boundary on every closed Legendrian submanifold of a closed contact manifold for every contact form. This conjecture was established in various settings by Cieliebak, Mohnke, Hutchings and Taubes, and others. In this talk, I will sketch a proof of the chord conjecture for conormal bundles of closed submanifolds of any closed manifold seen as Legendrians in the co-sphere bundle. This generalizes a result of Grove in Riemannian geometry regarding the existence of geodesics normal to the submanifold. The method of proof involves wrapped Floer cohomology with local coeﬃcients. This talk is based on joint work with Dylan Cant and Egor Shelukhin.

proof_stages.label: Contact and Reeb-chord framework
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up contact manifolds, Legendrians, Reeb vector fields and Reeb chords, recalls Arnol'd’s chord conjecture, and explains through basic cases why closedness of the ambient contact manifold matters.

proof_stages.label: Conormal-geodesic motivation and main theorem
proof_stages.summary: Orthogonal geodesics to a submanifold are related to Hamiltonian chords of its conormal Lagrangian and Reeb chords of its conormal Legendrian lift, leading to the theorem that every contact form on the unit cosphere bundle has a nonconstant chord on the conormal lift.

proof_stages.label: Gap left by previous chord-conjecture methods
proof_stages.summary: Prior results using symplectic homology, embedded contact homology, Rabinowitz Floer homology, or topological hypotheses are reviewed to show that none directly proves the full conormal-lift case, motivating the new local-system argument.

proof_stages.label: Wrapped Floer theory with tailored local systems
proof_stages.summary: The proof introduces local systems, defines wrapped Floer complexes for conormal Lagrangians with local coefficients, isolates the positive quotient generated by Reeb chords, and notes that if no Reeb chords exist then positive wrapped homology vanishes and total wrapped homology reduces to singular homology of the submanifold.

proof_stages.label: Wrapped Floer/path-space comparison
proof_stages.summary: An Abbondandolo–Schwarz type isomorphism, extended to local coefficients, identifies positive wrapped Floer homology of the conormal with relative homology of the path space, and its proof is sketched via Morse theory for the energy functional and a triangular half-strip Floer comparison map.

proof_stages.label: Relative Hurewicz topological input
proof_stages.summary: A relative Hurewicz theorem with local coefficients and the long exact sequence of the path-space fibration provide a first nonzero relative homotopy group for the pair consisting of paths with endpoints on the submanifold and constant paths.

proof_stages.label: Low-degree obstruction to absence of chords
proof_stages.summary: For disconnected path space or a non-surjective fundamental-group map, a specially chosen group-ring local system contradicts the no-Reeb-chord consequence that wrapped Floer homology equals the submanifold’s singular homology.

proof_stages.label: Higher relative homotopy forces a chord
proof_stages.summary: In the remaining case, the fundamental groups agree and the relative Hurewicz theorem with the group-ring local system recovers a nonzero higher relative homotopy group as relative path-space homology, hence positive wrapped Floer homology is nonzero and a Reeb chord must exist.

examples.name: Periodic Reeb flow on the standard three-sphere
examples.note: For the standard contact form on S^3, every Reeb orbit closes, so a chord exists trivially; this illustrates why the conjecture is nontrivial only for general Legendrians/contact forms.
examples.engagement_reason: The speaker uses periodicity of the Reeb flow on the standard three-sphere to verify directly that every point yields a closed orbit and hence a Reeb chord.

examples.name: Zero section in a one-jet space escaping in the Reeb direction
examples.note: The zero section in J^1M has no returning Reeb chords because the Reeb flow translates in the real coordinate, showing why compactness/closedness of the ambient contact manifold is a necessary hypothesis.
examples.engagement_reason: The speaker identifies the Reeb vector field in the one-jet space as translation in the real direction and concludes that the zero section escapes and has no returning chord.
